name: Buttermilk Bread
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons melted butter or butter substitute
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the buttermilk, stirring constantly until scalded. Remove from heat and let cool.
2. In a bowl, soften the yeast in lukewarm water. Add the sugar, salt, and melted butter to the yeast mixture. Gradually add flour until a medium batter forms. Beat until smooth.
3. Cover and let the batter stand in a warm place overnight.
4. Stir in enough flour to make a dough that is stiff enough to knead. Turn onto a lightly floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic.
5. Allow the dough to rise until doubled in size, then work it down slightly. Shape into loaves and place in well-oiled pans.
6. Cover and let the dough rise again until doubled in size.
7. Bake in a hot oven (425°F) for about 45 minutes. Makes 4 loaves.
Mrs. W.H. Juedeman, Bristow, OK.
